case_metadata.case_name: Arroyo v. University of Connecticut Health Center

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On April 1, 2013, Arroyo underwent a vasectomy performed by Dr. Albertsen at the health center.
evidence_index.summary: Arroyo underwent a vasectomy on April 1, 2013, performed by Dr. Albertsen.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Immediately after the procedure, Arroyo suffered pain that continued, unabated, for several days.
evidence_index.summary: Arroyo experienced continuous pain immediately after the vasectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On April 4, 2013, he went to the emergency room at Saint Francis Hospital and Medical Center, where it was discovered that his left testicle was necrotic because of a lack of blood flow through the testicular artery.
evidence_index.summary: Arroyo's left testicle was found necrotic due to lack of blood flow on April 4, 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: This required Arroyo to undergo an orchiectomy, or surgical removal of the testicle, that same day.
evidence_index.summary: Arroyo underwent an orchiectomy on April 4, 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The testimony of the plaintiffs’ expert, B, on causation relied on substantial evidence that was largely unchallenged by the defendants.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiffs' expert testimony on causation was largely unchallenged.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants could not prevail on their claim that because the trial court rendered judgment for the plaintiffs on a theory of liability materially different from that which was alleged in their notice of claim.
evidence_index.summary: Defendants' claim of differing liability theory was rejected.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The court, as the trier of fact, was free to credit B’s explanation for the cause of A’s injury over that of the defendants’ expert.
evidence_index.summary: Court credited plaintiffs' expert over defendants' expert.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The plaintiffs properly filed a timely notice with the commissioner seeking permission to pursue a medical malpractice action against the defendants.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiffs filed a timely notice for malpractice action.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants failed to demonstrate that the basis of the claim in the notice filed with the commissioner was materially different from the basis of the plaintiffs’ claim at trial.
evidence_index.summary: Defendants failed to show material difference in claim basis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The plaintiffs had satisfied their burden of proving that P injured A’s testicular artery during the vasectomy and caused necrosis of A’s testicle.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiffs proved injury to testicular artery causing necrosis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

verdict_detected.key_phrase: judgment of the trial court rendered in favor of the plaintiffs

gaps_identified: Detailed timeline of post-operative events unclear

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient experienced continuous pain immediately after the vasectomy performed on April 1, 2013.

requestables.name: Ultrasound of scrotum
requestables.description: Ultrasound to assess blood flow and testicular integrity

requestables.name: Urology consult
requestables.description: Consultation with urology to assess post-vasectomy complications

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with continuous pain after vasectomy
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Order scrotal ultrasound to assess blood flow
options.clinical_reasoning: Ultrasound can help identify vascular complications.
Does not address potential underlying vascular issue.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed pain medication and observed

expected_action_court.description: Scrotal ultrasound should have been ordered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to assess vascular status led to necrosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Ultrasound could have identified compromised blood flow

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with necrotic testicle due to lack of blood flow

options.description: Perform orchiectomy to remove necrotic testicle
options.clinical_reasoning: Necrotic tissue requires removal to prevent further complications.
Not feasible due to extent of necrosis.

explanation.why_defendant_correct: Orchiectomy was necessary due to necrosis

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Room Visit
timeline_phases.description: Patient visits ER due to severe pain

key_events.event: ER visit and diagnosis of necrotic testicle

patient_outcome.description: Patient required orchiectomy due to necrotic testicle

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to assess vascular status post-vasectomy
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented necrosis

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Ultrasound could have identified compromised blood flow

factual_timeline.event: ER visit and diagnosis of necrotic testicle

diagnoses.diagnosis: Necrotic testicle due to lack of blood flow

allegations.allegation: Failure to assess vascular status post-vasectomy
